AgentCardConfig.description: Agent identity and capabilities advertised via the A2A Agent Card.

stateTransitionHistory.deprecated:
This capability is not implemented in the A2A v1.0 spec and
was removed. Kept for backward compatibility only — value is ignored.
Default: false.
stateTransitionHistory.description: Enable state transition history capability advertisement.

AgentConfig.description:
Complete agent configuration for a2a-codex.

This is what a JSON config file (e.g. `agents/example/config.json`) maps to. All fields except `agentCard` are optional — sensible secure defaults are applied for everything.

codex.description: OpenAI Codex SDK connection and execution settings

mcp.description: MCP servers to register with Codex at startup. Keys are server names; values are stdio or http server configs. The key "a2a-subagents" is reserved for the sub-agent bridge.

events.description: Event transport configuration for sideband observability events.

memory.description: Optional memory configuration for persisting instructions and skills. When present, the materializer writes these to backend-specific paths in the workspace directory during executor initialization.

subAgents.description:
Optional sub-agents to expose as MCP tools via the `a2a-mcp-skillmap` bridge. When present, the parent agent spawns skillmap as a stdio MCP server and registers it under the reserved `a2a-subagents` key in the resolved  {@link  mcp }  map. Sub-agents declared here become callable as ordinary MCP tools by the Codex runtime.

When this field is absent or `agents` is empty, the parent skips every sub-agent code path with no side effects.

configDir.description: Directory containing the agent's config.json file. Populated automatically by the CLI scaffold when a config file path is provided. Used by the materializer for resolving relative paths in memory config.

EventsConfig.description:
Event transport configuration for sideband observability events.

Controls how trace events (MCP tool calls, agent reasoning, lifecycle) are delivered. Only the two built-in transports are configurable via JSON config — for custom transports (Kafka, Redis, DB, etc.), use the programmatic API and pass a transport function to `createA2AServer()`.

httpHeaders.description: Custom HTTP headers sent with every event POST. Useful for authentication tokens: ```json { "Authorization": "Bearer ${CORTEX_TOKEN}" } ```

httpTimeout.description: HTTP request timeout in milliseconds for the `"http"` transport.

httpUrl.description: HTTP endpoint URL for the `"http"` transport. Required when `transport` is `"http"`.

transport.description:
Built-in transport type.

- `"a2a"` — publish as sideband trace artifacts on the A2A   ExecutionEventBus (default, zero dependencies).
- `"http"` — POST events as JSON to  {@link  httpUrl } .

For custom transports, leave this unset and provide a transport function via the programmatic `createA2AServer()` API.

streamArtifactChunks.description: Stream artifact chunks individually (A2A spec-correct) vs buffer and send single artifact (inspector-compatible). Default: false (buffered).

emitReasoningSummaries.description: Publish reasoning summaries as thinking sideband events (default: true).

emitCommandEvents.description: Publish shell command start/end as tool_call sideband events (default: true).

emitFileChangeEvents.description: Publish file change metadata as decision sideband events (default: true).

McpHttpServerConfig.description: Configuration for an MCP server connected via HTTP (streamable).

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers sent with every request to this server. Use for auth tokens, API keys, etc. Values support `${ENV_VAR}` (or `$ENV_VAR`) substitution so secrets stay out of config.json — e.g. `{ "Authorization": "Bearer ${LINEAR_API_KEY}" }`.

url.description: URL of the remote MCP server (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8002/mcp)

McpServerConfig.description: MCP server configuration. Only stdio and http (Streamable HTTP) transports are supported. SSE-only servers are not supported — use http or stdio instead.

McpStdioServerConfig.description: Configuration for a stdio-based MCP server.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command. Values support `${ENV_VAR}` (or `$ENV_VAR`) substitution.

command.description: Command to launch the MCP server

env.description: Environment variables for the spawned process. Values support `${ENV_VAR}` (or `$ENV_VAR`) substitution so secrets stay out of config.json — e.g. `{ "API_KEY": "${MY_API_KEY}" }`.

MemoryConfig.description:
Optional memory configuration section in agent config.json.

Declares instructions and skills to materialize into the agent's workspace at startup. Both fields are optional — operators can declare instructions only, skills only, or both.

Paths are resolved relative to the directory containing config.json (Config_Directory) unless they are absolute.

instructions.description: Path to a markdown instructions file. Resolved relative to Config_Directory when relative, used as-is when absolute.

skills.description: Paths to skill directories, each containing a SKILL.md file. Resolved relative to Config_Directory when relative, used as-is when absolute.

advertiseHost.description: Hostname advertised in agent card URLs (default: "localhost"). Set to machine IP or "host.containers.internal" for Docker.

advertiseProtocol.description: Protocol used in advertised URLs (default: "http"). Set to "https" when the agent is deployed behind TLS or a TLS-terminating reverse proxy. The A2A spec requires HTTPS for production deployments.

cleanupInterval.description: Session cleanup interval in ms (default: 300_000 = 5 min)

reuseByContext.description: Reuse sessions by A2A contextId (default: true)

SkillConfig.description: A single skill exposed on the agent card.

SubAgentAuthConfig.description:
Outbound credentials the bridge presents when calling a remote A2A sub-agent. Forwarded verbatim into the bridge config consumed by `a2a-mcp-skillmap`.

`mode: "none"` is equivalent to omitting the `auth` field entirely and produces no auth block in the generated bridge config.

For `mode: "api_key"`, the optional `headerName` defaults to whatever `a2a-mcp-skillmap` uses (typically `X-API-Key`).

Tokens may reference environment variables via `${VAR_NAME}` syntax; the substitution happens during validation and missing variables cause the auth block to be omitted with a warning.

SubAgentConfig.description: One remote A2A agent the parent will expose as MCP tools via the `a2a-mcp-skillmap` bridge.

agentCardUrl.description:
URL of the sub-agent's A2A agent card. Required.

Typically `https://<host>/.well-known/agent-card.json`, but any URL the bridge can probe is valid. Must use the `http:` or `https:` protocol.

auth.description:
Outbound credentials the bridge presents when calling this sub-agent. Forwarded verbatim into the bridge config.

Omitting this field is equivalent to `{ mode: "none" }`.

endpointUrlOverride.description:
Optional override for the URL the bridge probes. Useful when the agent card declares an endpoint that differs from where the agent is actually reachable (NAT, port-forward, internal vs external).

When set, this URL is passed to the bridge instead of  {@link  agentCardUrl } . The bridge still resolves a card from this URL — point it at a card URL or a base URL it can probe under `/.well-known/`. Must use the `http:` or `https:` protocol.

name.description:
Stable identifier for this sub-agent. Becomes the prefix on generated MCP tool names (`{name}__{skillId}`). Required.

Allowed characters: ASCII letters, digits, hyphens, and underscores (must match `/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/`). Must be unique across all entries in the `subAgents.agents` array.

SubAgentsConfig.description:
Top-level shape of the `subAgents` field merged into the agent's `BaseAgentConfig`.

When this field is absent or `agents` is an empty array, the parent skips every sub-agent code path with no side effects (no bridge config written, no MCP entry injected).

agents.description: The list of remote A2A agents to expose as MCP tools.

SubAgentsOptions.description: Optional knobs that apply to the bridge process as a whole, not to any single sub-agent.

probeTimeoutMs.description: Probe timeout in milliseconds for the startup reachability check the parent performs against each sub-agent. Defaults to `5000` when unset.

responseMode.description: Skillmap response shaping mode passed through to the bridge config. Defaults to `"artifact"` when unset.

syncBudgetMs.description:
Sync budget in milliseconds passed to `a2a-mcp-skillmap` via the bridge config. Controls how long the bridge waits for an A2A agent to respond before switching to async task-handle mode.

When the budget expires the bridge immediately returns a `taskId` to the MCP client; the A2A dispatch continues in the background. The LLM can then poll via the built-in `task_result` / `task_status` tools which actively re-query the remote agent before responding.

- Set lower (e.g. `10000`) for interactive use where you want fast   task-handle responses.
- Set to `0` to wait indefinitely (no async fallback).
- Defaults to `30000` (30 s) when unset — skillmap's own default.

prompt.description: Timeout for a single prompt in ms (default: 600_000 = 10 min)

CodexConfig.description: OpenAI Codex SDK connection and execution settings.

workingDirectory.description: Absolute path to the Git repository Codex should operate on. Required at runtime. Supports ${WORKSPACE_DIR} and other ${ENV_VAR} substitution.

model.description: Model to use (e.g. "o4-mini", "o3", "gpt-4o"). Falls back to Codex default when omitted. Supports ${CODEX_MODEL} substitution.

sandboxMode.description:
Codex sandbox mode controlling filesystem access.
- "read-only" — analysis and review tasks; no file writes
- "workspace-write" — recommended default; writes scoped to workingDirectory
- "danger-full-access" — unrestricted; only use inside an isolated container or VM

approvalPolicy.description:
Tool approval policy.
- "never" — auto-approve all (default, required for headless A2A)
- "on-failure" — approve only on command failure
- "untrusted" — approve tools from untrusted sources
- "on-request" — NOT SUPPORTED in headless A2A

networkAccessEnabled.description: Allow Codex to make outbound network requests (default: false).

webSearchMode.description:
Web search access.
- "disabled" — no web search (default)
- "cached" — cached results only
- "live" — live search (requires networkAccessEnabled: true)

skipGitRepoCheck.description: Skip Codex's built-in Git repository validation check (default: false).

additionalDirectories.description: Additional directories to make accessible beyond workingDirectory. Paths support ${ENV_VAR} substitution.

developerInstructions.description: Instructions prepended to every Codex prompt as developer context.

baseUrl.description: Override the OpenAI API base URL (useful for corporate proxies).

codexPathOverride.description: Override the path to the Codex CLI binary.

configOverrides.description: Additional Codex configuration overrides (CodexOptions.config key=value pairs).

contextFile.description: Filename for the pre-built domain context file within workingDirectory (default: "context.md")

contextPrompt.description: Default prompt used when buildContext() is called without an explicit prompt.
